title: Bringing Back the Quagga
story: Scientists made a groundbreaking discovery on June 4, 1984. They successfully cloned DNA from an extinct animal called the quagga, a zebra-like creature that vanished over a century ago. This amazing feat marked the first time genetic material from a long-gone species was brought back to life in a lab. It opened up exciting possibilities for understanding and potentially reviving other extinct animals.
activity_question: What type of extinct animal did scientists clone DNA from?
follow_up_question: Why do you think scientists are interested in cloning extinct animals?

title: Cash at Your Fingertips
story: Cha-ching! Money became easier to access in 1973 when Don Wetzel, Tom Barnes, and George Chastain received a patent for the ATM on June 4. title: Rare Sky Show: Venus Transit Meets Eclipse
story: The sky put on an incredible double feature in 1769. First, Venus appeared as a tiny dot crossing the face of the Sun. Just five hours later, the Moon completely blocked out the Sun in a total solar eclipse. This super-short time between these two amazing events had never happened before and hasn't since. June 4 that year was truly a once-in-forever day for sky watchers!
activity_question: What two celestial events happened on the same day in 1769?
activity_choices: A Venus transit and a solar eclipse
A meteor shower and a lunar eclipse
A comet sighting and a supermoon
An asteroid flyby and a blue moon
activity_answer: A Venus transit and a solar eclipse
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's so rare for a Venus transit and solar eclipse to happen on the same day?

title: Sun's Fiery Giant Reaches for the Stars
story: The sun put on a spectacular show in 1946! Scientists spotted an enormous solar prominence, a massive loop of fiery gas erupting from the sun's surface. These solar fireworks remind us just how powerful and awe-inspiring our nearest star can be.

bonus_fact: Solar prominences are cooler than the sun's surface and can last for months!

title: Maldives Takes a Big Step Forward
story: Waves of change swept across the Maldives on June 4, 1964. This island nation in the Indian Ocean adopted its very first constitution. It was a huge moment for the country's people. The constitution laid out important rules for how the government would work. It marked the beginning of a new chapter in Maldivian history.
activity_question: What did the Maldives adopt in 1964?
follow_up_question: Why do you think having a constitution is important for a country?

title: Batman's Grandma Rocks the Charts
story: Holy hit single, Batman! In 1966, a quirky tune called 'Batman & His Grandmother' by Dickie Goodman climbed the music charts. This novelty song, released on June 4, blended the caped crusader's adventures with an unexpected twist - his grandma! It reached number 70, proving even superheroes can't escape family shenanigans.
activity_question: What was the name of the novelty song that climbed the charts?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine other superheroes with funny family members in songs?

title: A New TV Channel Lights Up Worcester
story: Lights, camera, action! In 1970, a new TV station flickered to life in Worcester, Massachusetts. WSMW TV channel 27 began broadcasting on June 4, bringing fresh entertainment to living rooms across the city. As an independent station, it offered unique shows and local programming, giving viewers more choices for their TV time.
activity_question: What type of TV station was WSMW channel 27?
bonus_fact: TV stations use numbers like 27 to show their place on the dial, making them easy to find.

title: A Magical World of Words Begins
story: Imagine a world where sky pirates soar and floating rocks hide secrets. Paul Stewart, the writer who'd bring these wonders to life, took his first breath in London on June 4, 1955. He grew up to create The Edge Chronicles and Far Flung Adventures, filling bookshelves with tales that transport young readers to fantastical realms full of excitement and imagination.

title: Elfquest Creator's Magical Beginning
story: Wendy Pini, the creative mind behind the beloved comic book series Elfquest, entered the world on June 4, 1951. Born in San Francisco, California, Pini would grow up to enchant readers with her fantastical stories and artwork. Her imagination and talent have inspired countless fans to explore magical realms through the pages of her comics.
